nodes.body: <<FILL role=setup words=118 beats='the city of Meridian at dusk, seen from the union hall stair above Central Station, where the elevated skyrail catches the last light; you, the newest junior member of the Cartographers' Union, summoned by the urgent bell; stationmistress Odal tells the hall plainly that the city's founding archive, the deeds, the water rights, and the orphan ledger, was lifted from the Charter Room three nights ago and rides tonight in a freight car of the Meridian Line night express, which leaves at twelve minutes past nine and crosses the border at dawn'>>

choices.label: Climb to the hall and hear the plan.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=112 beats='Odal raises the magistrate's writ of recovery so the whole hall can read the seal: the union acts tonight within the law; an adult officer must present the writ, and opening a sealed car takes the car's number plus one honest piece of cause; she names the union's safety law like a second seal, no child on a moving car's outside, ever, juniors read, count, signal, and speak while adults do all the climbing and carrying; the express calls at five viaduct stations, and its one long stop is the midnight water-stop at Aqueduct Junction, twenty-four minutes'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=104 beats='the map table; Odal chalks the night: three crews, one train, one night, working three vantages and converging on the water-stop, where she herself, stationmistress of Aqueduct Junction, will stand on the platform with the writ from twenty to midnight; a courteous purser named Venn signed the freight manifest without reading it, she says, and a frightened junior clerk rides with the cargo; the union recovers, it does not raid; nothing angrier than paper and lamplight, if the night goes right'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=132 beats='the sorting of the crews; Route-Warden Casse, dry and exact, leads the Planners from the Central switch tower: route boards, timetables, freight ledgers, the numbers that can open a sealed door; Rigger Bel Toma, calm as ballast, leads the Climbers along the fixed gantries to the junction's service cradle: adults ride the cradle, juniors belay, spot, and run the lanterns; Advocate Imma Rhee, who has never lost a parley, leads the Talkers aboard the express itself with honest tickets: the dining car, the purser's office, the conductor's parley; Odal asks where you will serve until dawn, and the choosing is yours'>>

choices.label: Join the Planners under Route-Warden Casse.
Join the Climbers under Rigger Bel Toma.
Join the Talkers under Advocate Imma Rhee.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='the switch tower over Meridian Central, all brass and lamp glass; the Meridian Line laid out on the route board as rows of small lights, the night express waiting below as lamp twelve; Casse hands out timetable cards like a dealer who has never lost'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the tower's standing rule etched over the desk, one reading is a rumor, two readings are a fact; the express's paperwork pulled and laid flat, freight ledger and wagon manifest side by side under the green lamp'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=122 beats='two documents and one hour before departure; the weight ledger, columns of axle loads off the yard scale, and the wagon manifest, cars and cargos and consignees; Casse says pick your thread, numbers first or names first, because a night this short only unravels from one end at a time'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=108 beats='ledger columns tallied with a card ruler, junior fingers walking the axle loads line by line; the express declares forty-one tons behind the tender, neat as printed music'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=128 beats='the tower's own tally from the yard scale reads nine tons over the declaration; somewhere in the express a car rides heavy and unclaimed; Casse's rule stares down from the wall, one reading is a rumor, two readings are a fact, while the departure bell rings across the yard; check the figure against the axle-counter at Copperline, or run with it now and buy a head start'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='waiting on the express to pass Copperline Viaduct at five past ten; the axle-counter's ticker tape spools into a junior's hands while Casse mans the lamps, the tower quiet as arithmetic'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=118 beats='the tape shows the overload riding one truck: freight car nine, third from the rear, and the heavy car has a number now; cause must reach Odal before the water-stop spends itself; the Circle Tram leaves in four minutes and reaches Aqueduct Junction by twenty to twelve, or the tower can lamp-code the finding ahead through the Saltglass relay and follow after'>>

choices.label: Ride the Circle Tram to the junction.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=112 beats='the tram across the sleeping city with the ledger page wrapped in oilcloth; rooftops and dark canals below, and away east the express's twin lamps threading the viaduct arches'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='Saltglass Arches slides past beneath the tram at 11:18 sharp, mail sacks swinging aboard the express in lamplight; the timetable holding like good masonry under the whole night'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=104 beats='Aqueduct Junction at twenty to midnight; Odal on the platform with the writ in its case, the water tower dripping, and at the siding a dray cart waiting with two bored porters and a stack of empty crates'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=132 beats='the express sighs in at three minutes to midnight and Venn steps down, walking his tickets; Casse's crew lays the ledger and the Copperline tape before Odal, cause and car number together; she asks the crew how the door should open: the writ presented to Venn cold and formal, or the juniors laying the arithmetic in front of him first, since Casse holds that numbers are kinder than accusations'>>

choices.label: Let Odal lead with the writ.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=118 beats='the writ presented under the platform clock; Venn reads it twice, hands not quite steady, and unlocks car nine's man-door with the smallest bow of a long-serving man'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=84 beats='lantern light in the freight car; crates of true glassware in the middle bay, and forward a bulkhead standing a hand-span proud; a junior finds the fresh paint line at the seam and six screw heads wearing rivet paint'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=122 beats='the porters' screwdrivers take the six painted thumb-screws and the panel comes away; behind it the green crates, and behind them Dorrin Sel stands up out of the dark with his lantern, younger than his coat, saying he can name the buyer if anyone will listen before the constable writes him down; Odal looks to the juniors who found the seam, her question plain: hear him now, or let the law go first'>>

choices.label: Ask Odal to hear him out.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=98 beats='Dorrin's story spilling out onto a mail crate: the forged manifest, the pay he never spent, the standing order from the Collector at the Gate House naming other cities' archives; the constable writes, Odal listens, and the water tower tops the boiler off above them all'>>

choices.label: Stand by while the crates open.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='the three green crates opened one by one on the platform: deeds, water rights, the orphan ledger with its ribbon intact; the express released to roll on lighter at 12:21 exactly; Dorrin to ride back with the union and tell the magistrate everything'>>

choices.label: See the archive onto the barrow.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=success words=104 beats='dawn from the union hall stair, the archive home under guard and Odal signing the recovery into the union log; Casse pins the Copperline tape above the tower desk as exhibit one of the lesson, two readings, one door, no shouting; the Collector's name is already wired to the border court before breakfast'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the junction constable, unhurried and correct, reads Dorrin his particulars with no hand laid on him; the clerk carries one crate to the barrow himself, which the constable allows, because dignity costs a recovery nothing'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=108 beats='the archive tallied crate by crate against the writ's schedule under the platform lamps; Venn surrenders his manifest copy unasked; the express rolls out at 12:21 with its whistle low, one lie lighter'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=112 beats='morning; the orphan ledger back in the Charter Room and the magistrate's clerk reading the tally aloud; Casse observes that the law was enough tonight because the numbers came checked; a quiet pride that holds all the way to sleep'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='the juniors spread the ledger and the Copperline tape on Venn's little desk; he follows the columns with a silver pencil, courteous as ever, complimenting the tallying like a man praising the rope that will tie him'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=128 beats='the silver pencil stops on car nine; Venn says softly that a purser signs a great many things, and forty years of service hang in the little office like a coat on its peg; the crew can press him gently, that helping the recovery makes a witness of him, or step back and let Odal's writ carry the weight so he need only stand aside'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=112 beats='the appeal made gently: a man who helps the recovery tonight stands in the witness column, not the warning column; Venn asks whether the union speaks for honest witnesses, and the juniors answer with Odal's own words from the hall'>>

choices.label: Walk with him to car nine.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='Venn takes down his ring of keys and walks the platform to car nine ahead of the writ, unlocking the man-door under Odal's eye and naming the false bulkhead himself, quietly, like a debt'>>

choices.label: Stand by as the panel falls.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=success words=98 beats='the panel down, the crates out, Dorrin found sitting among the deeds and treated gently; Venn writes his own statement in a round old hand; Casse tells the crew that arithmetic opened the door and mercy kept it open; dawn colors the viaduct as the archive rides home'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=104 beats='Odal presents the writ formally at the man-door with the arithmetic laid out as cause; Venn stands aside with a bow that looks a great deal like relief'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=118 beats='the panel unscrewed by the porters, the crates checked against the writ's schedule, Dorrin taken into the constable's care with his lantern still lit; everything by the book, and tonight the book is enough'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=118 beats='the recovery logged in a column of signatures, the express away at 12:21; Casse allows that there are nights when the quiet way is the fast way; the archive is home before the milk carts and the crew asleep before the gulls'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=84 beats='the tower's lamp-code lesson, quick and exact, long flash and short; Casse warns that nine and four differ by a single flash and that a tired eye will lose it'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=98 beats='the message away through the Saltglass relay: HEAVY CAR NINE HOLD CAUSE AT JUNCTION; the relay's answering flicker crosses the dark city like a struck match'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=118 beats='the crew follows on the quarter-past-eleven milk train, watching the junction answer the relay; the acknowledge comes back one long flash, but the repeat-back is smudged by chimney haze right on the car digit; protocol says a smudged repeat-back means send it all again, which costs ten minutes of the window; repeat the message in full, or trust that it went through'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='the message sent twice more from the Saltglass relay box, the junction's repeat-back coming clean at last: NINE, one long flash sitting exactly where it belongs'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the milk train limps into Aqueduct Junction at ten past midnight; the platform is already busy with lanterns, and none of the hurry looks like trouble'>>

<<FILL role=success words=108 beats='car nine stands open; Odal, with tape and ledger relayed true, presented the writ on the lamp-code cause at the top of the window; the green crates are on the barrow and Dorrin sits quiet beside the constable; Casse points from the junction lamp to the tower lamp and calls them one instrument; the archive rides the pilot engine home at one o'clock'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=108 beats='the milk train in at ten past midnight, and on the platform a wrongness: car four stands open under the lanterns, mail sacks and stove parts, Venn apologizing beautifully to everyone at once'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=132 beats='the smudged flash read four for nine, and the writ spent its first courtesy on the wrong door; Venn is now within his rights to ask better cause before another seal breaks, and nine minutes of window remain; Casse owns the mistake without blinking and lays out two doors: wire Palegate Rise to hold the express at the 2:40 stop, or board the express with Odal and ride the writ to the border gate'>>

choices.label: Board and ride for the border.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='the window closes; the express rolls at 12:21 with car nine sealed; the wire to Palegate Rise goes out clean, but a two-minute stop is a narrow door for a writ, a purser, and six painted thumb-screws'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=112 beats='Palegate Rise at 2:40; the express barely breathes; Venn, correct now to the letter, requires the full form, and the form takes four minutes the stop does not have; the tail lamps shrink toward the border hills'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=104 beats='dawn at the switch tower; the express crossed the border with the archive still hidden, and Odal files the writ with the border court before breakfast; Casse posts the smudged tape above the desk beside the rule it proves, and nobody shouts; the case will bring the crates home by a month of paperwork, and no junior of this crew will ever again trust a smudged repeat-back'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='riding the express as lawful passengers with Odal's writ in her case; the long dark run past Palegate Rise while the juniors re-prove the cause from the raw tape at a dining-car table'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=104 beats='by four in the morning the arithmetic stands solid; the conductor, shown the working, wires ahead to Termino Gate on his own authority, and the border wardens answer that the train will be held inside the gate'>>

<<FILL role=ending words=112 beats='at 5:41 the express stands held inside Termino Gate while wardens open car nine on the wired cause; the crates come home; but in the Gate House ledger the juniors glimpse the Collector's standing order naming three more cities' archives, and the recovery ends as something larger begins; the union copies the page before the morning ink dries'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=118 beats='the raw overage grabbed and run with, the Circle Tram caught at a sprint; the tower shrinking behind, the rule still on its wall, and the number warm and unchecked in a junior's pocket'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=84 beats='on the tram the juniors re-derive the car from memory against a borrowed timetable, and the scale column lands, uneasily, on car seven; nobody says the word rumor out loud'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=122 beats='Aqueduct Junction at twenty to midnight; Odal reads the unchecked figure pointing at car seven and her question is gentle and heavy: is this number checked twice, because a writ's first courtesy spends only once; the truthful answer is no; admit it and spend the window's first minutes re-checking against Venn's own scale book, or stand on the figure and open car seven'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=98 beats='the admission out loud and plain; Odal nods as if a door has passed a test, and Venn, asked courteously, produces his own scale book, because the request is small and honest'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='two juniors race the columns under the platform lamp while the express takes water; the mis-scaled line surfaces, a doubled decimal in the seventh column, and the corrected total lands on car nine'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the Copperline tape, wired ahead by the tower on its own initiative, arrives to second the correction; two readings at last, with four minutes of margin in the window'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=108 beats='the writ presented once and rightly; the man-door open, the false bulkhead found by its fresh paint line, the porters' screwdrivers busy while the boiler tops off'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=98 beats='the three green crates on the barrow at 12:19 and the express released on time; Dorrin walks to the lamp room between Odal and the constable with his dignity intact; Casse gives the crew the night's true ledger, that a mistake admitted at speed weighs less than a doubt carried to a door; the archive sleeps at home'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='car seven opened on the unchecked figure: milled glass and stove iron, honestly manifested; Venn's courtesy acquires a fine polish as he notes that the writ's cause is spent'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=112 beats='the water-stop ends with car nine sealed and the express rolling into the dark; on the emptying platform Casse takes the blame onto his own page in front of the crew, before anyone else can reach for it'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=118 beats='the long tram ride home; at the tower Casse chalks the rule bigger, one reading is a rumor, and beneath it the juniors chalk what it cost; the border court takes the case at dawn with the corrected arithmetic attached, and the archive will come home by law, later and lawyer-slow, with the crew there when it does'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='the wagon manifest read aloud down its column, coal, mail, milled glass; car nine listed GLASSWARE FRAGILE for Termino Gate, riding third from the rear'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=104 beats='the line read twice the tower way; glassware heavy enough to crush its springs, sealed at Central by a junior clerk's hand, and bound for a border gate at dawn'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=128 beats='the oddity found by a junior's finger: car nine's consignee line is blank, and freight for a border crossing never rides unclaimed; Casse goes very still, the way he does at a good number; the blank line is a thread with two ends: the telegraph office keeps duplicate waybills that could name who paid, or the blank line itself can ride straight to Odal as thin but honest cause'>>

choices.label: Trace it at the telegraph office.
Take the blank line to Odal.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=118 beats='the telegraph office off the concourse, warm with resin and dust; the night clerk glad of quiet company, pulling duplicate flimsies from the wire baskets under green glass'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=84 beats='car nine's booking on its flimsy: paid in coin, no sender named, routed care of the Gate House, Termino; a shipment ashamed of both its ends'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=118 beats='the same fist, the clerk says, has booked storage at the Gate House twice before, a standing order with its far end across the border; the finding can travel two ways: by official wire to the border warden, formal and slow but arriving with the telegraph seal's weight, or in the crew's own hands by Circle Tram to Odal before midnight'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=98 beats='the wire drafted with the clerk's help in proper form, GATE HOUSE CONSIGNMENT CAR NINE RECOVERY WRIT FOLLOWS, and away south under the telegraph seal'>>

choices.label: Ride the milk train after it.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='the crew reaches the junction on the milk train as the water-stop opens; Odal already holds the warden's answer, cause seconded from the border itself, heavy as a second signature'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='car nine opens to the writ without a raised voice; the false bulkhead, the green crates, and a tired clerk found sitting on the deeds with his lantern turned low'>>

choices.label: Pin the reply to the board.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=completion words=108 beats='crates home and Dorrin gently in hand; pinned to the union board a copy of the warden's reply, which Casse calls the rarest kind of night, one where the paperwork arrived before the trouble; the standing order goes to the magistrate to be unpicked city by city'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=108 beats='the flimsy wrapped in oilcloth and the tram across the roofs; Saltglass Arches below at 11:18 with its mail sacks swinging aboard the express, everything on time except the truth'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='the junction at twenty to twelve; Odal reads the Gate House routing and sets it beside the blank consignee line, two halves of one lie fitting like tally sticks'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=112 beats='the express in at three to midnight; the writ presented on doubled cause; Venn reads the flimsy and his courtesy folds into something older and sadder, and he unlocks the man-door himself'>>

<<FILL role=success words=104 beats='the false bulkhead down, the green crates out, the orphan ledger untied and counted page by page under the water tower; Dorrin names the Gate House without being asked twice; the express rolls on time and the archive rides the pilot engine home, with Casse granting that names, tonight, were faster than numbers'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='the tram to the junction with the manifest folded to the blank line; the aqueduct's dark arches keeping pace alongside, mile after quiet mile'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=104 beats='Aqueduct Junction at twenty to midnight; Odal at her platform desk with the writ in its case, and at the siding the dray cart waiting with two bored porters and a stack of empty crates sized to swallow other crates'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=132 beats='eleven fifty-seven and the express sighs in; the water bag swings out over the tender and twenty-four minutes go on the clock; Odal calls the blank line honest cause but thin, and a writ spends best with a second thread; stake the writ now and open car nine at once, or hold four minutes for the other crews' word, trusting the union's own plan to converge'>>

choices.label: Stake the writ on it now.
Hold for the crews to converge.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=118 beats='the writ presented on the blank line alone; Venn hesitates a half-breath too long and stands aside; the man-door opens on lantern dark and the smell of new paint'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=84 beats='the false bulkhead found and unscrewed, the crates there and true; but the car's far door swings on its hinges, and Dorrin is gone up the train among the passengers while the porters lift the panel down'>>

<<FILL role=ending words=112 beats='the archive comes home whole, which is the night's work done; but the clerk who could name the Collector reaches Termino in a borrowed seat among strangers, and the juniors learn that a recovery's second half rides on in the Talkers' country; on the union board the blank consignee line waits, half a name, half a question'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=98 beats='four minutes of held breath; then Toma's gantry lantern spells PANEL SEEN and a Talker runner brings Venn's manifest copy signed unread; the thin cause turns thick as rope'>>

choices.label: Present the writ on three threads.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='the writ presented once, on three crews' cause; car nine opens without argument to the panel, the crates, and Dorrin found sitting on the deeds with his lantern, too tired to run'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the tally run under the water tower while the boiler drinks; deeds, water rights, the orphan ledger with its ribbon; the express released at 12:21 to the second'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=98 beats='the convergence Odal chalked at dusk lands exactly at midnight with every crew's thread in the knot; the archive is home before two, and in the union log Casse writes the whole night up under one word, together, which from Casse is a speech'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=108 beats='the rigger's shed under the Central gantries; Bel Toma issuing harnesses sized small and checking every buckle twice; a wall of coiled lines, and her voice like slow water naming each junior's station for the night'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='the harness lesson given until it sits in the bones: two anchors or nothing, and the second anchor is the one that holds you; juniors belay, spot, and signal; only adults ride steel, tonight and every night'>>

choices.label: Follow Toma up the gantry stair.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=112 beats='the climb up the fixed stair to the gantry walk, clipped to the rail the whole way; the city opening below, canals like dropped ribbon, and far off the skyrail's twin lamps threading the dark'>>

choices.label: Walk the aqueduct service way east.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='the long walk east along the aqueduct service way, the old water-channel's maintenance path running arch for arch beside the skyrail; Saltglass Arches passes below at 11:18 with its mail-sack swing, right on the card'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=104 beats='arrival above Aqueduct Junction at half past eleven; the water tower, the siding with its waiting dray cart, Odal's lamp already on the platform, and the service cradle hanging from the tower gantry like a patient spider'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=122 beats='Toma at the cradle head with forty minutes in hand and one crew; two useful hours ago she would do both, tonight it is one: rig the cradle's line properly to her law, both anchors and full checks, or first walk the crew out the footbridge to scout the freight sightlines while the yard lamps last'>>

choices.label: Rig the cradle by the law.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=118 beats='the rigging done right and slow, two anchors set in separate steel and each tugged to a count of ten; juniors pay out the belay line from the platform winch drum, learning the brake lever's two positions by feel'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=84 beats='the test drop with a sandbag rider; the brake bites clean; Toma clips in as the express's whistle sounds off the western arches, 11:57 to the minute'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=98 beats='Toma rides the cradle down through lamplight to hang beside car nine's roofline; below, the platform business of water bags and tickets goes on, and the freight car's roof lies dark with one wired-shut ventilator cowl'>>

choices.label: Hold the cradle at the roofline.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=128 beats='Toma hangs level with car nine's roof, juniors on winch and lantern below; her hand-signals up the line offer two doors: the roof ventilator, a clean look into the car with a lantern relay from the platform, or the side inspection hatch, faster to reach but needing cradle slack right over the platform lamps where any porter might look up'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='the cradle walked two arm-lengths over the roofline; juniors angle the big platform lantern on Toma's finger-counts, light going up through the cowl in three short washes'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='through the vent Toma reads the car like a page: true glassware crated in the middle bay, and forward a bulkhead standing a hand-span proud of the frame where no bulkhead should stand'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=108 beats='the second pass catches it: a fresh paint line at the bulkhead seam, six screw heads wearing rivet paint, and the corner of a green crate stenciled white; cause, hanging thirty feet over the platform'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=118 beats='cause must travel from gantry to writ; the gantry lamp can code it to Odal fast and public, letters of light over the whole junction for anyone to read, or two runners can take the tower stairs down with Toma's sketch of the seam, slower and surer and quiet; the water bag has minutes left in it either way'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='the gantry lamp spelling PANEL SEEN CAR NINE in careful longs and shorts across the junction; Odal's platform lamp answers UNDERSTOOD, and heads begin to turn at the talking light'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=112 beats='among the turned heads, Venn's; the purser looks up the tower a long moment, then crosses the platform with his courteous walk while the water bag swings back off the tender'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=132 beats='Venn calls up the gantry stair, politely, that inspections of railway property want railway notice, and what is the union's business aloft; Toma's rule is that the union does not lie, and she leaves the answer to the crew on the platform: speak it straight down to him, writ and cause and car nine, or signal Odal to come and answer for the union with the writ in her hands'>>

choices.label: Let Odal answer with the writ.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='the answer given plain from the platform rail: a recovery under the magistrate's writ, cause seen through the ventilator, car nine; Venn's courtesy goes very still, like water finding its level'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=104 beats='Odal arrives with the writ before the silence ends; Venn, out-papered and strangely relieved, unlocks the man-door and asks only that the glassware be handled kindly'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=118 beats='the panel down and the three green crates in lantern light; Dorrin stands up out of the dark saying he never learned how to want this; the constable is gentle, and the boiler goes on topping off overhead'>>

choices.label: See the crates onto the barrow.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=success words=118 beats='the archive rolled up the platform as the express releases on time; Toma ropes the cradle home and tells the crew the night's truth, that the best rigging done tonight was an honest answer given at height; dawn finds the orphan ledger back in the Charter Room and the crew asleep in the shed among the coiled lines'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=84 beats='the gantry lamp spells ANSWER COMES; Odal crosses with the writ and the two adults speak under the tower while the crew holds the cradle line steady overhead, brake set, breath held'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=98 beats='from above, the parley reads like weather clearing; Venn's shoulders settle, keys come out, and the gantry sighting stands as cause while the man-door opens to the law'>>

choices.label: Bring Toma up and stand down.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=completion words=108 beats='panel, crates, and a frightened clerk gathered into careful hands; the crew's part done exactly and no more; Toma logs the sighting in the rigger's book and writes beneath it that a crew that knows which words are whose gets home by two, and they do'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='Toma's pencil sketch of seam and screws dropped down in a weighted bag; two juniors take the tower stairs with it, boots quiet, the sketch warm as a coal against the night'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=122 beats='on the half-landing, climbing fast with a lantern, Dorrin Sel himself, off the train for air or for escape, eyes gone wide at the sight of union harnesses; he stands between the runners and the platform, frightened enough to bolt and bury the panel deeper; speak to him gently from where you stand, no step closer, or ease back against the rail and lamp-flash the platform for the constable'>>

choices.label: Step back and flash for help.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the words kept simple and the distance kept wide: the union has a writ, the night has one good door left, and it is the one where he walks down first and tells it himself'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=108 beats='Dorrin comes down the stair ahead of the runners like a boy leaving a high dive, and at the bottom asks for Odal by name; the sketch changes hands on the way down'>>

choices.label: Stand by while he tells it.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=success words=104 beats='Dorrin opens the panel himself with his own screwdriver, naming the Gate House and the Collector while the constable writes; the crates come home with his testimony riding alongside; Toma tells the runners that the gentlest belay of the night was played out on a staircase, in words'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='backs to the rail and room left wide; the hand-lamp flashes the platform code for constable; Dorrin freezes, then simply sits down on the step and waits, tired past running'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=112 beats='the constable comes up the stair unhurried; particulars taken on the half-landing without a raised voice; the sketch goes down to Odal and the writ meets the man-door as the water bag swings clear'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=112 beats='the recovery unrolls in good order, panel and crates and tally, nobody cornered and nobody chased; in the rigger's book Toma writes that the crew held two lines at once tonight, one of hemp and one of patience, and neither slipped'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='the cradle slacked and walked toward the car's flank, into the wash of the platform lamps; Toma's shadow thrown ten feet tall across car nine's boards'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=104 beats='a porter looks up, then another; a murmur crosses the platform like wind over water, and Venn's head turns; Toma signals hold, hanging in plain lamplight with the hatch a body-length away'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=128 beats='seen is seen; Toma swings back to the gantry shadow and the crew reads the salvage: wait out the stir with the winch locked and buy the ventilator look late in the window, or drop the attempt now and carry the one thing already learned, that car nine's springs ride crushed, down to Odal as thin cause'>>

choices.label: Wait, then try the vent late.
Drop it and carry the springs.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=118 beats='minutes bought with stillness while the platform stir settles and the water bag does its slow work; 12:09 when Toma rides out again over the roofline'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=84 beats='the ventilator look, quick and clean this time: the proud bulkhead, the fresh paint line at the seam, a green stenciled corner behind it; cause, with the window thinning to minutes'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=118 beats='12:15 by the tower clock and the watering nearly done; on the platform Odal's lamp asks the gantry a single patient question, CAUSE; three minutes will not do everything: code the cause down by gantry lamp, fast and public and finished, or bring Toma to the platform in the cradle to speak it herself, surer in the saying but slower than the last of the water'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=98 beats='PANEL SEEN CAR NINE FRESH PAINT SIX SCREWS; the light steady, the letters clean, and Odal's answer already moving across the platform as the last word spells'>>

choices.label: Hold the light on the last word.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='the writ presented at the man-door on the lamp's word with ninety seconds of water left; Venn reads it and stands aside; the express will leave on time and without its secret'>>

<<FILL role=success words=98 beats='panel, crates, and a clerk found sitting on the deeds; the tally run under the tower as the express rolls at 12:21 exactly; Toma tells the winch crew that a lamp is a rope for words, and tonight they paid it out without a kink; the archive sleeps at home before two'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the cradle's slow descent to the platform, juniors braking by the count exactly as drilled; Toma steps off and walks to Odal, speaking low and exact with the sketchbook open'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=108 beats='the water bag swings clear as she finishes; the tower clock gives the window nothing more; Odal's hand rests on the writ while the engine builds its leaving breath'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=132 beats='Odal, stationmistress of her own junction, can hold a watered train a short while on her authority if the cause is sound, and she asks the gantry crew straight, in front of everyone: was it seen true; say the cause is sound and stake the union's word on two seconds of lamplight, or admit the look was thin through a half-shut cowl and let her weigh it honestly'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='the answer given steady, seam, screws, stencil, and the times read off the sketchbook; Odal reads faces the way Casse reads columns, and holds the Aurelia at the platform on the stationmistress's board'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=112 beats='the writ presented once; the man-door, the panel, the green crates in lamplight; Dorrin surrenders his screwdriver like a sword and looks mostly relieved'>>

<<FILL role=success words=118 beats='eleven minutes of held train and the archive on the barrow before the board flips; the express away short one lie; Toma tells the crew the night turned on their two seconds of looking and the nerve to call it sound out loud; the rigger's book gets a full page'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='the honest answer, two seconds and a lamp-wash; Odal nods, lets the Aurelia roll at 12:21, and has the junction wire Palegate Rise the sketch and the times before the tail lamps clear the arches'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=104 beats='Odal offers the pilot engine standing warm on the siding; the crew packs the small cab among lamps and coiled line, and the night country runs backward with the express's glow two arches ahead'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=122 beats='Palegate Rise at 2:38, but the home signal stands against the pilot on a fouled block and the two-minute stop spends itself out of reach; Odal names the last door, Termino Gate at dawn, where the writ still runs on the city side of the line; press on with her through the small hours to the border parley, or wire Termino the whole cause from here and turn for home, trusting the wardens to act on paper alone'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=118 beats='the long small hours behind the express, the pilot running on Odal's authority section by section; juniors sleeping in turns on coiled line while the border hills go grey'>>

choices.label: Stand at the gate at dawn.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=84 beats='Termino Gate at 5:41; the express held inside the gate by wardens reading Odal's writ under lanterns going pale; car nine's man-door opens on the city side of the border by the width of a platform'>>

<<FILL role=success words=108 beats='the false bulkhead down at sunrise, deeds and water rights and the orphan ledger lifted out while the Gate House windows watch behind drawn curtains; Dorrin, found in the dining car with his coat buttoned wrong, chooses the union's side of the platform; the longest road home carries the heaviest proof, and the crew rides back asleep on the crates'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=98 beats='the whole cause wired from the Palegate box, sketch described screw by screw; Termino acknowledges in a warden's steady fist, and the pilot turns for the city under a sky going slate'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='the shed at dawn, harnesses on their pegs; then the junction telegraph chattering: EXPRESS HELD GATE PANEL FOUND CRATES SAFE; breakfast gathers around the message like a stove'>>

<<FILL role=ending words=104 beats='the archive comes home on the noon train under a warden's seal, recovered by strangers who trusted a sketch; the crew learns the night's quiet lesson, that a true report travels faster than any crew can ride and does its climbing alone; in the rigger's book Toma pastes the wire itself, the only page written in someone else's hand'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the cradle roped home and the stairs taken down with the one hard fact the footplates gave for free: car nine's springs sit crushed flat under a cargo of blown glass; heavy is honest, but heavy is not a seam'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=108 beats='Odal hears it and measures it and shakes her head: weight is cause to wonder, not cause to open; she will not spend the writ's one courtesy on a maybe, and the window runs out with the crew grounded'>>

<<FILL role=ending words=112 beats='the express rolls with its secret; but the springs datum, wired to the border beside the Planners' ledger, is enough for the wardens to hold the train at dawn for a lawful look; recovery comes by relay, not by rescue, and the crew spends breakfast learning the difference and deciding they can live with it'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='out along the fixed footbridge that spans the through line, clipped to the rail hand over hand; below, the junction lies like a map of itself, platform, water tower, and the siding where the dray cart stands loaded with empty crates under a Gate House delivery board'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=112 beats='the scout's tally filling the sketchbook: the cart's crates sized to hide other crates, the porters bored and incurious, every sightline and lantern angle marked against the express's arrival'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='11:57 and the Aurelia in from the west; from the footbridge, car nine reads wrong three ways: springs crushed flat, vent cowl wired half-shut from inside, and a smudge of rivet-grey paint low on the forward door edge'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=128 beats='rich cause in the sketchbook, but the cradle still hangs unrigged and the window is spending itself; go back and rig by the law, both anchors and every check, arriving over the car late but right, or rig a single anchor and buy the scouting minutes back'>>

choices.label: Rig one anchor to save time.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=104 beats='the rigging done full and fast with no step skipped, Toma calling the checks and the juniors answering; the second anchor tugged to ten like always, because always is the point'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=118 beats='Toma out over car nine at 12:07 with the window half spent and the footbridge tally pinned to her sleeve; below, the water bag works its slow trade'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=118 beats='the wired cowl fought open a finger's width and a lantern wash sent inside; the proud bulkhead there, the paint line there; the footbridge cause and the cradle cause shake hands thirty feet up; minutes remain for exactly one telling: code the doubled cause to Odal by gantry lamp and let the writ walk, or bring Toma up and send her to the platform in person with the sketchbook, the surer, slower way'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=84 beats='the gantry lamp spells it twice for surety, cowl, seam, springs, smudge; Odal's answer crosses the platform at a stationmistress's walk with the writ already out of its case'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=98 beats='the man-door open at 12:16; porters' screwdrivers at the six painted screws; from above, the whole recovery fits in one lantern circle, panel, crates, and a clerk sitting down among the deeds like his knees had quit'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='the express released to the second; the crew off the steel by half past; at the siding the dray cart still waits for a transfer that now belongs to the constable's notebook'>>

<<FILL role=success words=98 beats='the archive home before two and the sketchbook page pasted into the rigger's book beside the times; Toma's verdict read aloud to the shed, that scouting bought the cause and the law's rigging spent it, which is the whole craft in one night; the crew sleeps like anchors'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='Toma up, unclipped, and down the stair with the sketchbook under her arm; the crew holds the gantry watch with lamps trimmed while the car below keeps its secret one more minute'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=108 beats='on the platform the sketchbook opens page by page for Odal and, deliberately, for Venn; the purser reads the smudge, the cowl, the springs, and his own signature on the manifest, and grows very quiet'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='Venn asks to see the door-edge himself, touches the tacky grey with one gloved finger, and stands aside before the writ is even raised; some doors open from the keeper's side'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=118 beats='the panel down and the crates tallied while the boiler tops; Dorrin gathered in gently, the constable's hand on his shoulder like a teacher's; the express away on time; in the rigger's book Toma writes that evidence walked downstairs tonight and did the talking, and the crew adds the grey fingerprint page as proof'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=112 beats='one anchor set and the second skipped, the scouting minutes singing in everyone's ears; Toma frowns at the arithmetic of it and lets the frown lose; the cradle swings out'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='halfway down, the line jumps its sheave with a crack like a knuckle; the cradle lurches and Toma swings hard, catches the gantry ladder with both hands, boots finding a rung, safe, shaken, forty feet of quiet below her'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=132 beats='nobody hurt and nothing dropped, and the platform gone still and listening under the tower; the mishap happened on Odal's junction, and the law says her equipment, her word; own it to her now, whole and at once, or quietly re-rig with both anchors and try to buy the look back before anyone asks'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=104 beats='the report made from the platform edge, single anchor, jumped sheave, no injuries; Odal grounds the cradle with one word and thanks the crew for bringing her the truth while it was still warm'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=118 beats='the crew finishes the night as lantern-bearers for other people's work, and from the platform watches the Talkers' parley carry the recovery through the man-door without them'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=108 beats='the archive comes home tonight, but not by the gantry; in the shed at dawn Toma sets the jumped sheave on the table where the second anchor should have hung and says only that the law is written in the shape of the nights it would have saved; the crew hears her, and next time the law will hold them forty feet above a platform, which is where laws live'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=84 beats='the second anchor going in with cold hands while the window drains; Toma stops mid-knot, looks at her own shaking fingers, and calls it herself: no, two anchors and no secrets, and she walks the crew down to tell it'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=98 beats='the truth laid out too late to buy the minutes back; the water bag swings clear as they finish, and the Aurelia rolls at 12:21 with car nine sealed and the gantry's look never taken'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=104 beats='the recovery passes to the border court's slow mill, the crates a month from home behind paperwork instead of thumb-screws; in the rigger's book Toma writes the whole of it, sheave, silence, and the cost of a quiet fix, and signs it; the crew learns that the failure that teaches rigging is cheaper than the one that teaches falling, and that both are dearer than telling'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='boarding the express at Meridian Central with honest tickets, 9:05 on the platform clock; Rhee in her plain good coat, the crew dressed as exactly what they are, junior mapmakers on a night errand'>>

choices.label: Find your seats before the whistle.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='Rhee's three parley rules given over the seat backs as the Aurelia pulls out at 9:12: ask before you accuse; a door opened by its keeper stays open; and never crowd a frightened man'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=122 beats='twelve cars of night train, and listening is the crew's whole craft until midnight; the dining car is where people talk over soup, and the mail vestibule is where paper talks off the hook; Rhee will float between, and the juniors pick their post'>>

choices.label: Take a table in the dining car.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=108 beats='soup and the click of viaduct joints; Copperline Viaduct's ninety-second stop at 10:05 with Venn walking tickets on the platform, courteous to every window in turn'>>

choices.label: Trade a look with the crew.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=128 beats='an off-duty brakeman two tables down, grumbling into his bread that car nine rides locked even against the purser, sealed at Central by a junior clerk with a manifest nobody countersigned; the grumble is a thread, and Rhee's first rule says ask before you accuse; draw the brakeman out with honest questions about his train, or keep the table quiet and send word back for Rhee to work him herself'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='honest questions about couplings and grades, the kind railway men answer for free; the brakeman warms, and the story arrives on its own: Venn signed for car nine sight unseen at Central, which in forty years he has never once done'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=112 beats='more, into his bread: the clerk who sealed the car, young Sel, rides with it and takes his meals standing at the freight door, and the brakeman reckons the lad has not slept since Central'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=118 beats='Rhee hears it and decides the parley opens in the purser's office before Saltglass; she asks the juniors which telling should open it: the brakeman's words, human and warm and hearsay, or the cold oddity the crew logged at boarding, car nine sealed shut yet listed for the water-stop siding on the corridor board'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='the purser's office, brass and blotting paper; Rhee lays the story out gently with no names attached, while the juniors watch the purser's hands instead of his face, the way she taught them at boarding'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=132 beats='Venn pours tea with perfect courtesy and his hands are wrong, too careful, a man carrying a full cup across forty years of service; Rhee's glance hands the moment to the juniors, her way of teaching; the unsigned consignee line lies on the desk between them: press him on it gently now, one honest question, or fold the papers and wait for Aqueduct, where Odal's writ can carry the weight instead'>>

choices.label: Fold and wait for the writ.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=104 beats='the question asked small and true: who is car nine for, and why would glassware ride ashamed of its own address; Venn sets down the pot and looks at the night going past the window'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=118 beats='the silence Rhee taught them never to break; inside it Venn says quietly that he did not ask because he was paid not to, and that he has been listening to that sentence all the way from Central'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=122 beats='Venn asks the office, not quite looking at anyone, what happens to a man who opens a door he was paid to keep shut; the truthful answer belongs to the union: say plainly, yourselves, that the union speaks for honest witnesses, or step out and fetch Rhee to set formal terms so the promise is a paper one'>>

choices.label: Fetch Rhee to set formal terms.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=84 beats='the answer given as Odal gave it in the hall: recovery, not revenge; witnesses stand in a different column from thieves, and the union reads its columns out loud'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=98 beats='Venn takes up his keys like a man picking up his own name; at Aqueduct Junction he walks past his own manifest to the man-door of car nine and opens it before Odal has the writ half out of its case'>>

<<FILL role=success words=112 beats='the false bulkhead named by the man who was paid to forget it; crates, ledger, and a frightened clerk gathered in gently; Venn gives his statement in a round old hand, and Rhee tells the crew that the second rule won the night, a door opened by its keeper stays open, and that they should write it somewhere they will lose it and find it all their lives'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='Rhee's terms, quiet and exact: witness standing offered and taken, everything said written down; the little office fills with the particular calm of things done properly'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='at the water-stop the writ, the manifest, and Venn's statement meet on Odal's platform desk like tributaries; the man-door opens to all three at once'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=98 beats='panel and crates and the orphan ledger counted twice; Dorrin taken into care without a hand raised; the express away on time; Rhee's lesson over cocoa at the junction, that mercy which gets written down survives the morning, and the morning is where recoveries live'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=108 beats='the papers folded and the tea finished politely; Saltglass Arches slides past at 11:18 with its mail swinging aboard; the office parley adjourned, not lost'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=128 beats='at 11:40 a junior on corridor watch sees lantern light through the freight-car door glass where no light should be, and Dorrin kneeling at the forward bulkhead with a screwdriver; the sighting is cause if it is witnessed properly, and the freight vestibule is inside the train and lawful to stand in; go forward and witness it through the glass at a distance, or hold post as posted and send the report back for Rhee to witness herself'>>

choices.label: Hold post and send for Rhee.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='the walk forward through sleeping cars, unhurried, tickets in hand like any passenger; the freight vestibule cold and loud with rail joints, the door glass a pane of black'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=118 beats='through the glass, plain as a lit stage: Dorrin at the forward bulkhead, screwdriver working a painted screw, three green crate-corners showing where the panel stands ajar, twelve minutes out of Aqueduct; he has not seen the watchers, and Rhee's third rule stands guard, never crowd a frightened man; slip back and send for Rhee so the witnessing becomes official, or open the vestibule door and speak first, kindly, before the junction crowds him with lanterns'>>

choices.label: Slip back and send for Rhee.
Open the door and speak first.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=112 beats='the report handed back in one taught sentence; Rhee comes forward alone, stands at the door glass a full minute, and writes what she sees in her level hand while the junction lamps rise ahead'>>

choices.label: Let the adults take the door.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='at the water-stop the writ meets a witnessed cause; Odal, Rhee, and the constable enter the freight car together, lanterns low and voices lower; Dorrin looks up from the half-open panel and simply sets the screwdriver down'>>

<<FILL role=success words=118 beats='the recovery whole by 12:15, panel, crates, ledger, and a clerk who never had to be chased; he tells the constable the watchers could have shouted and did not, and that this is why he stayed sitting down; Rhee tells the crew the kindest thing in the night was the distance they kept, which is a strange sentence to be proud of forever, and they are'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=104 beats='the vestibule door opens on the third rule breaking; the kindest voice in the crew says his name, and Dorrin comes up off his knees like a startled hare, lantern swinging shadows up the freight car walls'>>

choices.label: Hold still and try to calm him.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=118 beats='no chase, every junior stone-still, but he is past hearing; he jams the panel shut, pockets the loose screws, and is gone up-train into the passenger crowd before the junction's first lamp slides past the glass'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=108 beats='at the water-stop the panel hides its seam again and the cause is now a story about a boy who ran; Odal will not spend the writ on it, and the express rolls at 12:21 still carrying the archive; Rhee takes the blame onto her own page for posting juniors at a door, teaches the third rule again on the cold platform, and boards at Palegate to sit with Dorrin through the small hours; the crates come home days later on his testimony, bought at the price of one bad minute at a vestibule door'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=84 beats='the post held; the report travels the corridor hand to hand in Rhee's taught form, what, where, when, and nothing else; the train begins its long brake for the junction'>>

choices.label: Give your report at the door.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=98 beats='Rhee witnesses from the glass herself, then carries report and juniors to the platform where Odal stands with the writ; the sighting, timed and doubled, is cause with its boots on'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=104 beats='the freight car opened in good order, the panel, the crates, the clerk collected quietly at the far door where he had hoped to slip down; the tally done under the water tower by 12:18; Rhee's cocoa verdict, that a watch which holds its post writes half the recovery, and the half it writes is the half that holds in court'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='the corridor board's oddity laid on Venn's desk: a sealed through-car listed for the Aqueduct water-stop siding, which is a stop where engines drink, not where freight wanders'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=132 beats='someone plans a transfer at the water-stop, onto the dray cart the union has clocked at the siding all week; Venn's puzzlement over the amended board is the first unrehearsed thing his face has done all night; the guard's van telegraph can warn Odal now, minutes mattering, or the crew can verify first by having the brakeman pull and read the actual siding order before crying wolf on a forged board'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the guard's van telegraph tapping the warning south, TRANSFER PLANNED WATER STOP SIDING CAR NINE; Odal's acknowledgment comes back in a stationmistress's fist, one word, HELD'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=108 beats='Aqueduct Junction at 11:57; from the vestibule steps the crew sees the dray cart at the siding, two hired porters and empty crates sized to swallow other crates, exactly the shape of the warning'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='the constable strolls to the cart and asks the porters, politely, on whose order they wait; the paper they show names the Gate House, Termino, and the writ has its second thread'>>

<<FILL role=success words=112 beats='cause from the board and cause from the cart with the writ between them; car nine opens at 12:10, panel, crates, ledger, and Dorrin found sitting with the deeds; the porters, blameless and paid in advance, help load the archive onto their own cart for the ride to the strongroom, which even Odal allows is tidy; the express rolls one lie lighter'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=112 beats='the brakeman pulls the siding order from the van clip; real paper in a forged fist, the amendment written in the same hand as car nine's manifest copy, stroke for stroke'>>

choices.label: Match the hands line by line.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='careful minutes spent matching the forged fist while Saltglass falls behind; the proof turns solid as track, but the warning wire goes out short of Aqueduct with the junction already in sight'>>

<<FILL role=ending words=98 beats='the transfer never happens, the cart stands down, and the water-stop passes in watchfulness rather than recovery, the panel unfound tonight; but the forged order in the union's hands is the court's favorite exhibit by Friday, the one paper tying clerk, board, and Gate House in a single fist; the crew learns that verifying can cost an evening and win a case, and that both halves of that sentence are true at once'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=104 beats='the word passed on a folded napkin in Rhee's taught form; she drifts to the brakeman's table with her coffee and her country manners, and the juniors keep the table and the watch'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=118 beats='Rhee agreeing with the brakeman about grades and about management, in that order; the story arriving on its own, that Venn signed for car nine sight unseen at Central, which in forty years he has never once done'>>

choices.label: Hold the table and log the room.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=84 beats='Rhee works the grumble gently while the crew logs the room, who rides which car, who checks the corridor board, who eats standing; the dining car turns into a map with soup on it'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=122 beats='Rhee slides back with the story confirmed and hands the juniors the counting work that wins parleys: walk the corridor and count the sealed cars against the board, looking for seals that disagree with themselves, or sit tight and map who moves between cars in the hour before the junction'>>

choices.label: Count the seals on the cars.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=98 beats='the corridor walked end to end with tickets in hand, counting like mapmakers, seals against listings; most cars agree with themselves, the way honest paper does'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='car nine does not: a yard seal crimped over a company seal, double-sealed the way nothing honest ever is, and the crimp's date three nights old, the Charter Room's bad night'>>

choices.label: Time it and write it down.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the count written in the taught form, timed and signed by two juniors, handed to Rhee as the brakes come on for Aqueduct; on the platform it passes from her hand to Odal's'>>

choices.label: Present the count at the junction.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=completion words=118 beats='a doubled seal with the right bad date is cause a magistrate can love; the writ opens car nine at 12:08 and the night unpacks in order, panel, crates, ledger, clerk; Rhee gives the crew the whole cocoa sermon in one line, that counting is the humblest kind of courage and the hardest to argue with'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=108 beats='the aisle mapped from the corner table in small pencil marks; passengers ebb, the brakeman naps, and at 11:20 and again at 11:44 Dorrin Sel passes forward with his lantern, checking on something that owns him'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='the marks make a shape: he checks the panel on the quarter hour before every station; a frightened man is a clock, and the crew has read his time'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=112 beats='Rhee reads the notebook, taps the 12:15 mark that has not happened yet, and carries the prediction to the platform for Odal to spend as she sees fit'>>

choices.label: Watch the quarter hour prove you right.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=ending words=108 beats='the adults choose to watch rather than open, and at 12:15 Dorrin walks his circuit into a ring of quiet lanterns and stops, caught by arithmetic; but the water runs out before writ and panel and tally can all be done, and the express rolls with the archive aboard for the border wardens, wired ahead, to lift out at dawn on the prediction's proof; the crew discovers their notebook riding south in the mail like a small lawman, doing the work by post'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='the mail vestibule off the baggage car, waybills on the hook swinging with the grade; the mail clerk sorting under green glass, glad of quiet company that touches nothing'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=104 beats='the waybills read from the hook without a finger laid on them, mapmaker discipline; coal, mail, milled glass, each sheet agreeing with itself until car nine's does not'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=128 beats='car nine's waybill reads wrong to a junior's nose before it reads wrong to the eye, fresh ink on an old form, a copy passing as an original; the copied waybill is a thread with two pulls: carry it straight to Rhee and let the parley have it while the night is young, or ask the mail clerk's leave to set it against his duplicate book, where every original leaves its shadow'>>

choices.label: Ask to check the duplicate book.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=118 beats='the copied waybill carried back through three swaying cars, held flat in a timetable so the night air cannot crease the evidence'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=84 beats='Rhee reads it twice and holds it to the lamp where the form's watermark should sit and does not; her face does the thing it does before a strong parley, which is nothing at all'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=118 beats='Rhee reads the copied waybill twice, holds it to the lamp where the watermark should sit and does not, and asks the juniors, straight-faced, for counsel, because talkers who never weigh are only mouths: open the parley early at Saltglass and spend surprise while the thieves think themselves unseen, or hold to the union's clock and land everything at Aqueduct where writ, stationmistress, and constable stand waiting'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=98 beats='the office parley opened at 11:10 with the copied waybill on the desk; Venn's courtesy survives it, barely; up the corridor a door closes too carefully, a frightened clerk gone to check his panel off schedule'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='Rhee neither chases nor raises her voice; she posts herself in the corridor between Dorrin and nowhere, coffee in hand, and talks to the air about writs and witnesses and boys who get one good door a night'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='at Saltglass the mail swings aboard under lamplight; by the time the arches fall behind, Dorrin is sitting across from Rhee with his lantern out, telling it in order, and Venn is writing his own statement unasked'>>

<<FILL role=success words=104 beats='the express reaches Aqueduct with its confession already made; the writ is nearly a formality, the panel opened by the clerk who built its secret, the crates tallied by half past midnight; Rhee's verdict, that surprise is a tool you may use exactly once a night, and she is glad it was spent on mercy; the archive rides home with two witnesses who chose the union's side of the aisle'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=108 beats='certainty chosen; hours in hand, and Rhee spends them the way planners spend towers, on preparation; the crew learns what a parley rehearsal is by being in one'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=132 beats='one hour left and two right answers; Rhee can drill the crew by playing Venn, taking his courteous evasions in turn until every answer has an answer, or the crew can build the witness sheet, every observation of the night written timed and signed, the paper spine of a parley; both win differently'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='Rhee plays Venn better than Venn, tea-pour and all; the crew learns where the courtesies bend and which question has no courteous answer, the blank consignee asked with the copied waybill already on the desk'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=112 beats='at the water-stop the parley runs like a timetable, question, paper, question; Venn's answers arrive exactly where the rehearsal said they would, and he sets his keys on the desk between them himself'>>

choices.label: Walk with him to car nine.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=success words=112 beats='the man-door, the panel, the green crates in lamplight; Dorrin gathered gently at the far door; the express away at 12:21 with the tally already signed; Rhee tells the crew that every easy-looking parley is a rehearsed one, and lets them carry the writ's case back to Odal's office as the fee'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='the witness sheet built line by disciplined line, boarding to now, every seal, listing, and careful hand, each entry timed and signed by two; talkers' work that planners would frame'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=104 beats='at the junction the sheet lies beside the writ on Odal's platform desk and makes the cause look like what it is, a night's honest watching; Venn reads the twentieth line, the one holding his own unread signature, and stands aside without a word'>>

choices.label: File the sheet with the writ.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=completion words=98 beats='car nine opens to paper it cannot argue with; panel, crates, orphan ledger home; the magistrate later staples the witness sheet to the writ itself as the model of cause brought right, and Rhee reads the union's juniors the court's one-line compliment at the next hall meeting, twice, because some pay is worth hearing twice'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=118 beats='permission asked properly and granted; the duplicate book opens under the green lamp, every original's carbon shadow kept in order of booking'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=84 beats='car nine's true shadow is there, and it is not the hook's copy: the original waybill was made out to the Gate House, Termino, consignee the Collector, booking paid in coin at Central'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=122 beats='the carbon shadow is cause, but it lives in the clerk's book and the book is his charge; the differences run six lies long, hook against book, and the clerk has gone quiet and angry in his tidy way, forged paper being personal in a mail car; copy the page by hand with him watching, slow and sure, keeping his trust and his book aboard, or ask the bolder thing, that he close the book, bring it, and testify himself at the water-stop'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=98 beats='the page copied in a mapmaker's careful print, every figure checked back twice, the clerk initialing each line as true copy; slow work with the junction growing in the window'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='Rhee carries the initialed copy forward; at the water-stop it lies beside the writ, and the clerk's initials give it a spine no courtesy can bend'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='Venn reads the Gate House line and the coin-paid booking and asks no questions at all; the man-door opens at 12:11 with the water bag still swinging overhead'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=118 beats='panel, crates, ledger, clerk; the recovery in good order and the mail car's book never once out of its keeper's hands; Rhee's cocoa lesson runs that evidence borrowed politely is evidence twice, once for what it says and once for how it was got, and the second half is the half that lasts'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=108 beats='the bold ask, made respectfully; the mail clerk looks at his book, at the forged hook copy, and shuts the book with a snap that means yes; forgery in a mail car, he says, is a thing a man testifies about'>>

choices.label: Walk him through what to expect.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='Rhee walks him through the platform parley like a conductor calling stations; the juniors carry his flimsy box and his umbrella, which he brings, because dignity'>>

choices.label: Stand with him at the desk.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=112 beats='at Odal's platform desk the clerk lays the open book beside the hook copy and says, for the record, which one the mails stand behind; the writ does the rest while the boiler drinks'>>

<<FILL role=success words=108 beats='car nine opens on the word of the man whose job is knowing which paper is true; panel, crates, the orphan ledger untied under the tower lamp; Dorrin, hearing the mails themselves had spoken, gives up the Gate House name before he is asked; Rhee tells the crew that the strongest voice in any parley is a witness who came because he was asked well, and the clerk rides home with the archive like a man escorting the post'>>
